title: Anthropic says its latest AI model is too powerful for public release and that it broke containment during testing
description: Anthropic said Claude Mythos is too good at hacking and that's why you won't be able to use it anytime soon.
fullText:
Anthropic said its next-generation AI model is too powerful for the public.
 That's why Claude Mythos won't be publicly released, Anthropic said.
 Anthropic said Mythos demonstrated concerning capabilities, including the ability to breach its own safeguards.

 What are "high-severity vulnerabilities"?

 Who are the partners in "Project Glasswing"?

 Why is transparency crucial for AI safety?

 How does Mythos enhance cybersecurity?

 What is a "Mythos-class model"?

 HOME

title: Mercor hit with 5 contractor lawsuits in a week over data breach
description: The lawsuits accused the AI training firm of violating data privacy and consumer protection laws.
fullText:
Contractors said in at least five lawsuits that AI training startup Mercor exposed their data to hackers.
 Mercor said last week it was "impacted" by compromised LiteLLM software.
 One of the suits seeks to hold LiteLLM, security audit firm Delve, and others liable.

title: Iranian hackers' targeting of US critical infrastructure has escalated since start of war, US says
description: Iranian hacking campaigns targeting equipment¬Ýused across multiple U.S. critical infrastructure sectors are escalating in response to hostilities, U.S. cybersecurity,
fullText:
April 7 (Reuters) - Iranian hacking campaigns targeting equipment used across multiple U.S. critical infrastructure sectors are ‚Äåescalating in response to hostilities, U.S. cybersecurity, law ‚Äåenforcement and intelligence agencies said on Tuesday.

The hackers are targeting publicly exposed ‚Äãprogrammable logic controllers and supervisory control and data acquisition displays, according to the advisory. The targeted devices are used to interact with or control certain critical infrastructure-related equipment and systems, ‚Äåthe advisory said.

The ‚ÅÝhackers are seeking to cause ‚Äúdisruptive effects within the United States,‚Äù according to the advisory. ‚ÄúIn a ‚ÅÝfew cases, this activity has resulted in operational disruption and financial loss."

The warning comes as President Donald Trump warned that ‚Äúa ‚Äãwhole civilization ‚Äãwill die tonight‚Äù if Iran ‚Äãfails to make a ‚Äådeal with the U.S., while Iran said it would attack additional infrastructure targets across its Gulf neighbors.

In some cases the hackers interacted with data files in the systems to alter display data, while also extracting device project data, according ‚Äåto the advisory.

The hacking operations ‚Äãtargeted unnamed critical infrastructure organizations in ‚Äãthe government services and ‚Äãfacilities, water and wastewater systems and energy ‚Äåsectors, according to the advisory.

The ‚Äãadvisory was issued ‚Äãby the FBI, the National Security Agency, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, the Environmental Protection Agency, ‚Äãthe Department of ‚ÄåEnergy and U.S. Cyber Command‚Äôs Cyber National Mission Force.

The ‚ÄãFBI declined additional comment.
